title: potentilla lycopodioides var. scandularis
intro: Potentilla lycopodioides var. scandularis, also known as five-fingered cinquefoil, is a variety of the Potentilla lycopodioides plant, a flowering perennial native to the mountainous regions of eastern Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Potentilla lycopodioides var. scandularis include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections, as well as supporting the immune system. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it can also be used in the form of a tincture or dried powder for various health remedies.
